title: Over 30% of papers submitted on arXiv read as AI-written
excerpt: We scored the full text of 12,750 arXiv papers and found that about a third of new ones read as machine-written. Here is the method, the results, and an honest account of the limitations.
content_html:
We scored the full text of 12,750 arXiv papers and found that about a third of new ones read as machine-written. Here is the method, the results, and an honest account of the limitations.

A false-positive floor

There is a genre of headline that says "N% of X is now AI," and most are not worth reading, because the detector behind the number also flags some share of genuine human writing. If a tool marks 40% of new papers as machine-written but also marks 20% of papers written before ChatGPT existed, the real story is the 20% nobody mentioned.

So we built the study around that objection. Our detector, described here, is calibrated for academic writing; at a 0.4% false-positive rate it clears 99.6% of genuine pre-LLM scientific text and recovers 85% of AI academic text. We made that false-positive rate the anchor. We took papers submitted in 2021 and 2022, before ChatGPT, treated them as ground-truth human, and set the flag threshold so that exactly 0.4% of them trip it. That line is the floor. Every number we report is a share of papers above a threshold where genuine pre-LLM writing sits, by construction, at 0.4%. The pre-ChatGPT years then act as a built-in control: if the rise were an artifact of the detector, 2021 and 2022 would flag as high as 2026. The first figure shows they do not.

What we measured

We sampled ten field groups, roughly 25 papers per field per month, from January 2023 to July 2026, plus eight control months across 2021 and 2022, for 12,750 papers in total. For each one we pulled the version-1 PDF, so a paper revised in 2026 cannot leak modern text back into its 2023 slot. We scored the full body text instead of the abstract, because abstracts understate the signal: we have seen the same paper score under 20% on its abstract and over 70% on its body. Every reported figure carries a bootstrap 95% confidence interval.

Results

The flagged share is flat at 0.4% through 2021 and 2022, lifts off within months of ChatGPT, and climbs in two waves to about 32% over the most recent complete quarter, peaking near 39% in early 2026. The spread across fields is large, and it is the table and the second figure that carry it. The values below are each field's flagged share over the 12 months to July 2026, alongside its pre-LLM control level.

Field groupPre-LLM controlRecent flagged share95% CI
Computer science0.2%65.0%[59.3, 70.3]
Quantitative biology3.5%56.3%[51.0, 61.7]
Electrical eng. & systems1.7%51.3%[46.0, 57.0]
Economics & finance2.5%47.0%[41.3, 52.7]
Applied physics1.3%34.0%[29.0, 39.7]
Statistics1.8%31.3%[26.0, 36.7]
Condensed matter0.0%24.0%[19.3, 29.0]
High-energy physics0.5%14.0%[10.0, 18.0]
Astrophysics0.0%10.7%[7.3, 14.3]
Mathematics0.0%0.7%[0.0, 1.7]

Computer science leads at about 65%. Mathematics is lowest, near 0.7%, and the limitations section explains why its low value is hard to interpret. The control column is each field's 2021 to 2022 flag rate averaged over three sensitivity settings; the fields that rise most are not the ones with the highest pre-LLM control level, so an elevated starting point does not explain the rise.

Limitations

Control sample size. Each field's pre-ChatGPT control is 200 papers. At a 0.4% flag rate only eight papers flag across the entire 2,000-paper control, spread thinly over ten fields, so a single-threshold per-field control rate is coarse. The pooled floor is well estimated and is what the study is anchored to, but the per-field control levels are only approximate, and a larger control would not fix this: pinning a fraction-of-a-percent rate per field would require thousands of control papers per field that pre-2023 arXiv volume does not contain.

A low score can indicate low adoption or a detector blind spot. Mathematics is the clearest case. Mathematics papers are dominated by notation and theorem-proof structure, and once equations and references are removed the remaining prose is sparse and unlike the scientific English the detector was trained on. A mathematics paper drafted with heavy model assistance may score low because its prose is out of distribution for the detector, so a low score in mathematics is weak evidence that a human wrote the paper. The result is consistent with two very different explanations, lower adoption or reduced detector sensitivity in that register, and this data cannot separate them. The fields with the strongest in-distribution assumption, the prose-heavy ones, are also the ones that rise most, so this confound does not account for the aggregate trend. But in the low-scoring fields the ranking should be read as a lower bound on adoption.

Detector coverage. The detector is more sensitive to some generators than others, and we cannot evaluate it against the exact, private mixture of models and prompts that authors actually use. Incomplete coverage lowers the flag rate, so the reported prevalence is a lower bound: the true share is at least what we measured. The detector write-up reports the per-generator performance.

A flag is not authorship. The detector estimates whether text reads as machine-written, at a calibrated probability with a known error rate. It cannot separate a lightly-edited document from a wholly-generated one, and a single score is never grounds to accuse a specific person. We report the prevalence of machine-like writing, which includes heavy AI-assisted editing.

Try it

The detector is cheap to run and we make no money from it. You can try it for free on any arXiv paper here, and on your own text here.

blocks.html_en: We scored the full text of 12,750 arXiv papers and found that about a third of new ones read as machine-written. Here is the method, the results, and an honest account of the limitations.

sentences.en: We scored the full text of 12,750 arXiv papers and found that about a third of new ones read as machine-written.
sentences.zh: 我们对 12,750 篇 arXiv 论文的全文进行了评分，发现大约三分之一的新论文是机器编写的。

sentences.en: Here is the method, the results, and an honest account of the limitations.
sentences.zh: 这是方法、结果以及对局限性的诚实说明。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Share of new arXiv papers flagged as machine-written, 2021 to 2026, at a threshold calibrated so pre-ChatGPT papers flag at 0.4%. The eight slate points are the pre-LLM control months; the band is a bootstrap 95% interval.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Share of new arXiv papers flagged as machine-written, 2021 to 2026, at a threshold calibrated so pre-ChatGPT papers flag at 0.4%.
sentences.zh: 2021 年至 2026 年，新的 arXiv 论文被标记为机器撰写的比例，按照经过校准的阈值，因此 ChatGPT 之前的论文标记为 0.4%。

sentences.en: The eight slate points are the pre-LLM control months; the band is a bootstrap 95% interval.
sentences.zh: 八个石板点是法学硕士预科控制月份；该带是 bootstrap 95% 区间。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Share of papers flagged as machine-written by field, over the 12 months to July 2026 (about 300 papers per field).</figcaption>

sentences.zh: 截至 2026 年 7 月的 12 个月内，按领域标记为机器撰写的论文比例（每个领域约 300 篇论文）。

sentences.en: A false-positive floor
sentences.zh: 假阳性底线

blocks.html_en: There is a genre of headline that says "N% of X is now AI," and most are not worth reading, because the detector behind the number also flags some share of genuine human writing. If a tool marks 40% of new papers as machine-written but also marks 20% of papers written before ChatGPT existed, the real story is the 20% nobody mentioned.

sentences.en: There is a genre of headline that says "N% of X is now AI," and most are not worth reading, because the detector behind the number also flags some share of genuine human writing.
sentences.zh: 有一种标题说“X 的 N% 现在是人工智能”，大多数都不值得阅读，因为数字背后的检测器也标记了一些真正的人类写作。

sentences.en: If a tool marks 40% of new papers as machine-written but also marks 20% of papers written before ChatGPT existed, the real story is the 20% nobody mentioned.
sentences.zh: 如果一个工具将 40% 的新论文标记为机器编写的，同时也将 20% 在 ChatGPT 存在之前编写的论文标记为机器编写的，那么真正的情况是无人提及的 20%。

sentences.en: So we built the study around that objection.
sentences.zh: 因此我们围绕这一反对意见进行了研究。

sentences.en: Our detector, described here, is calibrated for academic writing; at a 0.4% false-positive rate it clears 99.6% of genuine pre-LLM scientific text and recovers 85% of AI academic text.
sentences.zh: 此处描述的我们的检测器针对学术写作进行了校准；以 0.4% 的误报率，它清除了 99.6% 的真实 LLM 预科科学文本，并恢复了 85% 的 AI 学术文本。

sentences.en: We made that false-positive rate the anchor.
sentences.zh: 我们将假阳性率作为锚点。

sentences.en: We took papers submitted in 2021 and 2022, before ChatGPT, treated them as ground-truth human, and set the flag threshold so that exactly 0.4% of them trip it.
sentences.zh: 我们在 ChatGPT 之前收集了 2021 年和 2022 年提交的论文，将它们视为真实的人类，并设置了标志阈值，以便其中只有 0.4% 的人会触发该阈值。

sentences.en: That line is the floor.
sentences.zh: 那条线就是地板。

sentences.en: Every number we report is a share of papers above a threshold where genuine pre-LLM writing sits, by construction, at 0.4%.
sentences.zh: 我们报告的每个数字都是超过阈值的论文比例，按结构计算，真正的法学硕士预科写作占 0.4%。

sentences.en: The pre-ChatGPT years then act as a built-in control: if the rise were an artifact of the detector, 2021 and 2022 would flag as high as 2026.
sentences.zh: 然后，ChatGPT 之前的年份充当内置控制：如果上升是探测器的假象，那么 2021 年和 2022 年将高达 2026 年。

sentences.en: The first figure shows they do not.
sentences.zh: 第一张图表明他们没有。

sentences.en: What we measured
sentences.zh: 我们测量了什么

blocks.html_en: We sampled ten field groups, roughly 25 papers per field per month, from January 2023 to July 2026, plus eight control months across 2021 and 2022, for 12,750 papers in total. For each one we pulled the version-1 PDF, so a paper revised in 2026 cannot leak modern text back into its 2023 slot. We scored the full body text instead of the abstract, because abstracts understate the signal: we have seen the same paper score under 20% on its abstract and over 70% on its body. Every reported figure carries a bootstrap 95% confidence interval.

sentences.en: We sampled ten field groups, roughly 25 papers per field per month, from January 2023 to July 2026, plus eight control months across 2021 and 2022, for 12,750 papers in total.
sentences.zh: 我们从 2023 年 1 月到 2026 年 7 月对 10 个领域组进行了抽样，每个领域每月大约 25 篇论文，加上 2021 年和 2022 年的 8 个控制月，总共 12,750 篇论文。

sentences.en: For each one we pulled the version-1 PDF, so a paper revised in 2026 cannot leak modern text back into its 2023 slot.
sentences.zh: 对于每一篇文章，我们都提取了版本 1 PDF，因此 2026 年修订的论文无法将现代文本泄漏回其 2023 年插槽中。

sentences.en: We scored the full body text instead of the abstract, because abstracts understate the signal: we have seen the same paper score under 20% on its abstract and over 70% on its body.
sentences.zh: 我们对全文而不是摘要进行评分，因为摘要低估了信号：我们看到同一篇论文的摘要得分低于 20%，正文得分超过 70%。

sentences.en: Every reported figure carries a bootstrap 95% confidence interval.
sentences.zh: 每个报告的数字都带有引导 95% 置信区间。

sentences.en: Results
sentences.zh: 结果

The values below are each field's flagged share over the 12 months to July 2026, alongside its pre-LLM control level.

sentences.zh: 计算机科学领先，约为 65%。

sentences.en: Limitations
sentences.zh: 局限性

sentences.zh: 控制样本量。

sentences.en: Each field's pre-ChatGPT control is 200 papers.
sentences.zh: 每个领域预ChatGPT控制为200篇论文。

sentences.en: At a 0.4% flag rate only eight papers flag across the entire 2,000-paper control, spread thinly over ten fields, so a single-threshold per-field control rate is coarse.

sentences.en: The pooled floor is well estimated and is what the study is anchored to, but the per-field control levels are only approximate, and a larger control would not fix this: pinning a fraction-of-a-percent rate per field would require thousands of control papers per field that pre-2023 arXiv volume does not contain.

sentences.en: A low score can indicate low adoption or a detector blind spot.
sentences.zh: 低分可能表明采用率较低或存在检测器盲点。

sentences.en: Mathematics is the clearest case.
sentences.zh: 数学是最明显的例子。

sentences.en: Mathematics papers are dominated by notation and theorem-proof structure, and once equations and references are removed the remaining prose is sparse and unlike the scientific English the detector was trained on.
sentences.zh: 数学论文以符号和定理证明结构为主，一旦方程和参考文献被删除，剩下的散文就很稀疏，不像检测器所训练的科学英语。

sentences.en: A mathematics paper drafted with heavy model assistance may score low because its prose is out of distribution for the detector, so a low score in mathematics is weak evidence that a human wrote the paper.
sentences.zh: 在大量模型辅助下起草的数学论文可能得分较低，因为其散文对于检测器来说不符合分布，因此数学得分低是人类撰写论文的证据不足。

sentences.en: The result is consistent with two very different explanations, lower adoption or reduced detector sensitivity in that register, and this data cannot separate them.
sentences.zh: 结果与两种截然不同的解释一致，即该寄存器中采用率较低或检测器灵敏度降低，并且该数据无法将它们分开。

sentences.en: The fields with the strongest in-distribution assumption, the prose-heavy ones, are also the ones that rise most, so this confound does not account for the aggregate trend.
sentences.zh: 分布假设最强的领域，即以散文为主的领域，也是上升最多的领域，因此这种混杂因素并不能解释总体趋势。

sentences.en: But in the low-scoring fields the ranking should be read as a lower bound on adoption.
sentences.zh: 但在低分领域，排名应被视为采用率的下限。

blocks.html_en: Detector coverage. The detector is more sensitive to some generators than others, and we cannot evaluate it against the exact, private mixture of models and prompts that authors actually use. Incomplete coverage lowers the flag rate, so the reported prevalence is a lower bound: the true share is at least what we measured. The detector write-up reports the per-generator performance.

sentences.en: Detector coverage.
sentences.zh: 探测器覆盖范围。

sentences.en: The detector is more sensitive to some generators than others, and we cannot evaluate it against the exact, private mixture of models and prompts that authors actually use.
sentences.zh: 检测器对某些生成器比其他生成器更敏感，我们无法根据作者实际使用的模型和提示的精确、私人组合来评估它。

sentences.en: Incomplete coverage lowers the flag rate, so the reported prevalence is a lower bound: the true share is at least what we measured.
sentences.zh: 不完整的覆盖率会降低标记率，因此报告的患病率是一个下限：真实的比例至少是我们测量的。

sentences.en: The detector write-up reports the per-generator performance.
sentences.zh: 检测器记录报告每个生成器的性能。

blocks.html_en: A flag is not authorship. The detector estimates whether text reads as machine-written, at a calibrated probability with a known error rate. It cannot separate a lightly-edited document from a wholly-generated one, and a single score is never grounds to accuse a specific person. We report the prevalence of machine-like writing, which includes heavy AI-assisted editing.

sentences.en: A flag is not authorship.
sentences.zh: 旗帜不是作者身份。

sentences.en: The detector estimates whether text reads as machine-written, at a calibrated probability with a known error rate.
sentences.zh: 检测器以已知错误率的校准概率来估计文本读起来是否为机器编写的。

sentences.en: It cannot separate a lightly-edited document from a wholly-generated one, and a single score is never grounds to accuse a specific person.
sentences.zh: 它无法将经过轻微编辑的文档与完全生成的文档区分开来，并且单个分数永远不能成为指责特定个人的理由。

sentences.en: We report the prevalence of machine-like writing, which includes heavy AI-assisted editing.
sentences.zh: 我们报告了机器写作的盛行，其中包括大量人工智能辅助编辑。

blocks.html_en: The detector is cheap to run and we make no money from it. You can try it for free on any arXiv paper here, and on your own text here.

sentences.en: The detector is cheap to run and we make no money from it.
sentences.zh: 该探测器运行成本低廉，我们不从中赚钱。

sentences.en: You can try it for free on any arXiv paper here, and on your own text here.
sentences.zh: 您可以在此处的任何 arXiv 论文以及此处的您自己的文本上免费试用。

content_html_zh:
我们对 12,750 篇 arXiv 论文的全文进行了评分，发现大约三分之一的新论文是机器编写的。 这是方法、结果以及对局限性的诚实说明。

假阳性底线

有一种标题说“X 的 N% 现在是人工智能”，大多数都不值得阅读，因为数字背后的检测器也标记了一些真正的人类写作。 如果一个工具将 40% 的新论文标记为机器编写的，同时也将 20% 在 ChatGPT 存在之前编写的论文标记为机器编写的，那么真正的情况是无人提及的 20%。

因此我们围绕这一反对意见进行了研究。 此处描述的我们的检测器针对学术写作进行了校准；以 0.4% 的误报率，它清除了 99.6% 的真实 LLM 预科科学文本，并恢复了 85% 的 AI 学术文本。 我们将假阳性率作为锚点。 我们在 ChatGPT 之前收集了 2021 年和 2022 年提交的论文，将它们视为真实的人类，并设置了标志阈值，以便其中只有 0.4% 的人会触发该阈值。 那条线就是地板。 我们报告的每个数字都是超过阈值的论文比例，按结构计算，真正的法学硕士预科写作占 0.4%。 然后，ChatGPT 之前的年份充当内置控制：如果上升是探测器的假象，那么 2021 年和 2022 年将高达 2026 年。 第一张图表明他们没有。

我们测量了什么

我们从 2023 年 1 月到 2026 年 7 月对 10 个领域组进行了抽样，每个领域每月大约 25 篇论文，加上 2021 年和 2022 年的 8 个控制月，总共 12,750 篇论文。 对于每一篇文章，我们都提取了版本 1 PDF，因此 2026 年修订的论文无法将现代文本泄漏回其 2023 年插槽中。 我们对全文而不是摘要进行评分，因为摘要低估了信号：我们看到同一篇论文的摘要得分低于 20%，正文得分超过 70%。 每个报告的数字都带有引导 95% 置信区间。

结果

到 2021 年和 2022 年，该比例将持平于 0.4%，在 ChatGPT 发布后的几个月内开始上升，并在最近一个完整季度中分两波攀升至 32% 左右，并在 2026 年初达到接近 39% 的峰值。 跨领域的分布很大，承载它的是表格和第二个数字。 下面的值是截至 2026 年 7 月的 12 个月内每个领域的标记份额及其预法学硕士控制水平。

现场组法学硕士预科控制最近标记的分享95% CI
计算机科学0.2%65.0%[59.3, 70.3]
定量生物学3.5%56.3%[51.0, 61.7]
电气工程& 系统1.7%51.3%[46.0, 57.0]
经济与金融2.5%47.0%[41.3, 52.7]
应用物理学1.3%34.0%[29.0, 39.7]
统计数据1.8%31.3%[26.0, 36.7]
凝聚态物质0.0%24.0%[19.3, 29.0]
高能物理0.5%14.0%[10.0, 18.0]
天体物理学0.0%10.7%[7.3, 14.3]
数学0.0%0.7%[0.0, 1.7]

计算机科学领先，约为 65%。 数学最低，接近 0.7%，限制部分解释了为什么其低值难以解释。 控制列是每个字段 2021 年至 2022 年标记率在三个敏感度设置上的平均值；上升最多的领域并不是法学硕士预科控制水平最高的领域，因此起点升高并不能解释上升的原因。

局限性

控制样本量。 每个领域预ChatGPT控制为200篇论文。 在 0.4% 的标记率下，在整个 2,000 篇论文控制中只有 8 篇论文标记，分散在十个字段中，因此每个字段的单阈值控制率是粗略的。 合并下限经过很好的估计，也是本研究的锚点，但每个领域的控制水平只是近似的，更大的控制水平并不能解决这个问题：将每个领域的百分之几的比率固定将需要每个领域数千篇控制论文，而 2023 年之前的 arXiv 卷不包含这些论文。

低分可能表明采用率较低或存在检测器盲点。 数学是最明显的例子。 数学论文以符号和定理证明结构为主，一旦方程和参考文献被删除，剩下的散文就很稀疏，不像检测器所训练的科学英语。 在大量模型辅助下起草的数学论文可能得分较低，因为其散文对于检测器来说不符合分布，因此数学得分低是人类撰写论文的证据不足。 结果与两种截然不同的解释一致，即该寄存器中采用率较低或检测器灵敏度降低，并且该数据无法将它们分开。 分布假设最强的领域，即以散文为主的领域，也是上升最多的领域，因此这种混杂因素并不能解释总体趋势。 但在低分领域，排名应被视为采用率的下限。

探测器覆盖范围。 检测器对某些生成器比其他生成器更敏感，我们无法根据作者实际使用的模型和提示的精确、私人组合来评估它。 不完整的覆盖率会降低标记率，因此报告的患病率是一个下限：真实的比例至少是我们测量的。 检测器记录报告每个生成器的性能。

旗帜不是作者身份。 检测器以已知错误率的校准概率来估计文本读起来是否为机器编写的。 它无法将经过轻微编辑的文档与完全生成的文档区分开来，并且单个分数永远不能成为指责特定个人的理由。 我们报告了机器写作的盛行，其中包括大量人工智能辅助编辑。

Try it

该探测器运行成本低廉，我们不从中赚钱。 您可以在此处的任何 arXiv 论文以及此处的您自己的文本上免费试用。
